quiz_title: AL — Motor Abilities - Types of Coordination [EN]
description: The concept of coordination, its types, and its role in motor learning and sporting performance.

questions.text: What is coordination within the system of motor abilities?
questions.explanation: Coordination is the ability to execute movements in a precise, purposeful, and harmonised manner.

options.text: The ability of muscles to produce maximal force
The ability to execute movements in a harmonised manner
The ability to sustain prolonged physical load
The magnitude of joint range of motion

questions.text: Which factor plays a central role in the functioning of coordination?
questions.explanation: Coordination is based on the controlling and regulating function of the nervous system.

questions.text: Which of the following belongs to the coordinative abilities?
questions.explanation: Balance ability is one of the important forms of coordinative abilities.

questions.text: What does rhythm sense mean in movement coordination?
questions.explanation: Rhythm sense is the ability to organise and time movements temporally.

options.text: The ability to organise and time movements temporally

questions.explanation: Spatial orientation ability supports the perception of the body's position and movement in space.

options.text: The perception of the body's position and movement in space

questions.text: What is the essence of reactive ability?
questions.explanation: Reactive ability is the capacity to respond to stimuli with a rapid and appropriate movement response.

options.text: A rapid and appropriate response to a stimulus

questions.text: Developing coordination is particularly important in the early stages of motor learning.
questions.explanation: True. Coordination supports the formation, refinement, and automation of new movement patterns.

questions.text: Why is coordination important for sporting performance?
questions.explanation: Good coordination enables more efficient, precise, and economical movement execution.

options.text: Because it substitutes for strength on its own
Because it enables more precise and economical movement
Because it is only important in endurance sports

questions.text: Which type of exercise most effectively develops coordination?
questions.explanation: Varied exercises involving direction changes and decision-making situations develop coordination well.

options.text: Monotonous running at a constant pace

questions.text: Which statement most accurately describes the relationship between coordination and technique?
questions.explanation: Coordination is the neural and movement-regulation foundation of technical execution.

options.text: Coordination and technique are completely independent
Coordination is an important foundation of technical execution
Technique is solely a matter of strength

questions.text: Coordinative abilities can only be developed during childhood.
questions.explanation: False. They are especially well developed in childhood, but can also be improved later with appropriate practice.

questions.text: Why can coordination deteriorate under the influence of fatigue?
questions.explanation: Fatigue impairs neural regulation and the precision of movements.

options.text: Because coordination does not depend on the nervous system
Because the precision of movement regulation decreases
Because joint range of motion increases
Because the athlete automatically becomes faster

questions.text: Which coordinative ability is most closely related to maintaining and regaining body posture?
questions.explanation: Balance ability plays a role in stabilising and regaining body posture.

questions.text: Which statement is correct regarding the sport-specific nature of coordination?
questions.explanation: Coordination is built on general foundations but is truly expressed in sport-specific movement patterns.

options.text: Coordination is completely identical across all sports
Coordination also manifests in sport-specific movement patterns
Coordination is only important in ball games
Coordination cannot be developed through sport-specific exercises

questions.text: Developing coordination can contribute to injury prevention.
questions.explanation: True. Better movement control and balance can reduce the risk of injury resulting from faulty movements.
